epics.title: Add deterministic action wrappers for common supervisor steps

tasks.title: Add a browser screenshot action wrapper
acceptance_criteria: The repo exposes an action wrapper that opens a URL, waits for load completion, and saves a screenshot to a requested path.
The action uses the existing browser automation tooling rather than introducing a second browser stack.
Usage is documented in the repo-local action documentation.
done_definition: A deterministic screenshot command exists under actions/ and can be invoked from the repository root.

tasks.title: Add wrappers for changed-file linting and route-plus-validate
acceptance_criteria: The repo exposes an action wrapper that targets lint to changed files or explicit file arguments.
The repo exposes an action wrapper that runs supervisor validate and route in one step.
Both actions are documented so agents can prefer them over ad hoc shell sequences.
done_definition: The common verifier and supervisor commands are accessible through repo-local actions.

epics.title: Strengthen design and review protocol contracts

tasks.title: Require screenshot evidence in design review outputs
acceptance_criteria: The design review schema requires explicit preview or screenshot evidence references.
Design critic instructions and documentation explain that screenshot evidence is expected for implementation review.
Validation fails when review outputs omit the required evidence field.
done_definition: Design review outputs cannot be considered complete without screenshot evidence.

tasks.title: Add visual constraints to design artifacts
acceptance_criteria: The design schema supports explicit visual constraints alongside UX rules and builder constraints.
Design-related docs and templates instruct agents to capture aesthetic constraints in structured form.
Existing design artifacts can validate after the new field is added.
done_definition: Visual constraints are part of the required design contract.

epics.title: Make refinement loops traceable in run state

tasks.title: Add lightweight revision and refinement tracking to run state
acceptance_criteria: Run state can record revision count and refinement notes without breaking existing runs.
The supervisor exposes a simple way to mark a run as entering refinement again.
Routing logic respects pending refinement instead of incorrectly staying done.
done_definition: A run can move from done back into refinement with traceable notes.

tasks.title: Update workflow rules and docs for screenshot-backed design refinement
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md states that design changes are not complete until browser evidence is captured.
The MVP documentation lists the new actions and refinement capability.
The instructions remain additive and aligned with the current MVP architecture.
done_definition: Repo documentation reflects the updated workflow requirements.

assumptions: Small additive scripts and schema updates are sufficient for this iteration; no full orchestrator rewrite is needed.
The repository can depend on existing local tools such as agent-browser and git for the new action wrappers.
Existing historical run artifacts do not all need to be backfilled immediately as long as schema compatibility is preserved where practical.
risks: Adding required schema fields can invalidate older artifacts if the migration path is not handled carefully.
A changed-files lint wrapper may miss intended files if git status parsing is too narrow or shell portability is poor.
Refinement tracking can become confusing if the route logic and documentation disagree about when to reopen a run.
